children.heading: Purpose
children.label: a Purpose
children.text: The purpose of this section is to direct the Secretary to further the commercialization of renewable energy and energy efficiency technologies through a five-year program.

children.heading: Demonstration and commercial application projects
children.label: b Demonstration and commercial application projects
i
children.text: the production and sale of electricity, thermal energy, or other forms of energy using a renewable energy technology;

children.label: ii
children.text: increasing the efficiency of energy use; and

children.label: iii
children.text: improvements in, or expansion of, facilities for the manufacture of renewable energy or energy efficiency technologies.

children.heading: .—Requirements
children.label: B .—Requirements
children.text: Each project selected under this section shall include at least one for-profit business. Activities supported under this section shall be performed in the United States. Each project under this section shall require the manufacture and reproduction substantially within the United States for commercial sale of any invention or product that may result from the project.

children.label: A
children.text: In supporting projects selected under subsection (c), the Secretary may choose from among the forms of agreements described in .section 13541 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: In supporting projects selected under subsection (c), the Secretary may also enter into agreements with private lenders to pay a portion of the interest on loans made for such projects.

children.heading: Cost sharing
children.label: 3 Cost sharing
children.text: Cost sharing for projects under this section shall be conducted according to the procedures described in section 13542(b) and (c) of this title.

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretary of Commerce;

children.label: ii
children.text: the National Laboratories of the Department of Energy;

children.label: iii
children.text: the Solar Energy Research Institute;

children.label: iv
children.text: the Electric Power Research Institute;

children.label: v
children.text: the Gas Research Institute;

children.label: vi
children.text: the National Institute of Building Sciences;

children.label: vii
children.text: the National Institute of Standards and Technology;

children.label: viii
children.text: associations of firms in the major renewable energy manufacturing industries; and

children.label: ix
children.text: associations of firms in the major energy efficiency manufacturing industries.

children.label: B
children.text: Not later than 18 months after , the Advisory Committee shall provide the Secretary with a report assessing the implementation of the program under this section, including specific recommendations for improvements or changes to the program and solicitation process. The Secretary shall transmit such report and, if any, the Secretary’s recommendations to the Congress.October 24, 1992

children.label: A
children.text: Not later than 9 months after , the Secretary shall solicit proposals for projects under this section. The Secretary may make additional solicitations for proposals if the Secretary determines that such solicitations are necessary to carry out this section.October 24, 1992

children.label: B
children.text: A solicitation for proposals under this paragraph shall establish a closing date for receipt of proposals. The Secretary may, if necessary, extend the closing date for receipt of proposals for a period not to exceed 90 days.

children.label: i
children.text: the need for Federal involvement to commercialize the technology or speed commercialization of the technology;

children.label: ii
children.text: the potential for the technology to have significant market penetration;

children.label: iii
children.text: the potential energy efficiency gains or energy supply contributions of the technology;

children.label: iv
children.text: potential environmental improvements associated with the technology;

children.label: v
children.text: the export potential of the technology;

children.label: vi
children.text: the likelihood that the proposal is technically sufficient to achieve the objective of the solicitation;

children.label: vii
children.text: the degree to which non-Federal financial participation is involved in the proposal;

children.label: viii
children.text: the business and financial history of the proposer or proposers; and

children.label: ix
children.text: any other factor the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: Conversion of cellulosic biomass to liquid fuels.

children.label: B
children.text: Ethanol and ethanol byproduct processes.

children.label: C
children.text: Direct combustion or gasification of biomass.

children.label: D
children.text: Biofuels energy systems.

children.label: E
children.text: Photovoltaics, including utility scale and remote applications.

children.label: F
children.text: Solar thermal, including solar water heating.

children.label: G
children.text: Wind energy.

children.label: H
children.text: High temperature and low temperature geothermal energy.

children.label: I
children.text: Fuel cells, including transportation and stationary applications.

children.label: J
children.text: Nondefense high-temperature superconducting electricity technology.

children.label: K
children.text: Source reduction technology.

children.label: L
children.text: Factory-made housing.

children.label: M
children.text: Advanced district cooling.

children.label: A
children.text: consider each proposal’s ability to meet the criteria developed pursuant to paragraph (1)(C); and

children.label: B
children.text: attempt to achieve technological and geographic diversity.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary for carrying out this section $50,000,000 for fiscal year 1994.
